notes: Measure Word: ê. A child is eⁿ-á until able to walk, then is called gín-á.
Refers to health only; used with lâng / pak-tó· / thâu-khak.
At end of a clause means "ran away"; otherwise usually cháu-chhut-khì.
For "next door neighbor" use keh-piah ê lâng (#20).
Always at the end of a clause. Frequently used with chhin-chhiūⁿ / ná chhin-chhiūⁿ.

english: to carry / lift / raise (a large bulky object with both hands or on the shoulders)
notes: Variant giah is now rare; also "to lift/hold something by means of a stick-like object" (Meas. ki).

english: to carry or hold an umbrella
to become feverish / to have a fever
the next room / the next house
the next village / a neighboring village

poj: keh-piah koh keh-piah / keh-piah ê keh-piah
poj_sandhi: keh2-piah2 koh2 keh2-piah / keh2-piah ê7 keh2-piah
taiwanese_hanji: 隔壁閣隔壁 / 隔壁的隔壁

english: even if / no matter how much ... still
to cry / cry about / cry because
for example / for instance; suppose that
notes: Nā is often used with these expressions.
Of persons (prone to illness) or of things such as vehicles (unreliable). Opposite of ióng.
ū gín-á usually has the same meaning.

english: to hold (a person / animal) in one's arms like an infant
at the roadside / on the side of the road

notes: A door on the side of a building, or any non-main door on the front, including a small door built into the large main door.

english: at the side of a person
lazy / be too lazy to / get lazy
to take a walk / take a stroll

notes: Sometimes synonymous with kheh-khì, sometimes with sió-sim (#67).

english: to be born / to give birth to

notes: When sè-jī means "careful" it is interchangeable with sió-sim.
